name: Barry Manilow
abstract: Barry Manilow ( MAN-il-oh; born Barry Alan Pincus; June 17, 1943) is an American singer and songwriter with a career spanning over sixty years. His hit recordings include "Could It Be Magic", "Looks Like We Made It", "Mandy", "I Write the Songs", "Ready to Take a Chance Again", "Can't Smile Without You", "Weekend in New England", and "Co…

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: would I be interested in doing a Mercer album with Barry Manilow? ... Barry’s delightful to work with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bernard Purdie
about_quotes.evidence: I had to tell Barry Manilow — you want Steve Gadd? I’ll go call him... I was there when you were nothing. I was there when you were doing demos.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bernard Purdie interviewed by Monk Rowe, West Orange, New Jersey, July 30, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Jillean Williams
about_quotes.evidence: And someone who has sort of come back is Barry Manilow, who has done a wonderful big band album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jillean Williams interviewed by Milt Fillius and Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 20, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Ronnie Zito
about_quotes.evidence: I did some recordings with him. I did some in New York and some in L.A.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ronnie Zito interviewed by Monk Rowe & Rick Montalbano, via Zoom, March 9, 2022
